title: Ruyschiana pinnata
intro: Ruyschiana pinnata, also known as Rhus pinnata, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, commonly used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Ruyschiana pinnata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ruyschiana pinnata is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and is traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to support wound healing and reduce pain.
taxonomy: Ruyschiana pinnata is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is commonly known as the pinnate parsley fern or Ruysch's parsley fern. The classification of Ruyschiana pinnata includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its order as Apiales.
